'''Howard V. Chaykin''' (born [[October 7]], [[1950s|1950]] in Newark, New Jersey) is a comic-book writer and artist. He illustrated the first ten issues of the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] comics series.

His earliest works were science-fiction and fantasy titles in the early 1970s for [[Wikipedia:DC Comics|DC Comics]]. Although his work on the ''[[Star Wars]]'' series was a major success, Chaykin is perhaps best known for the series he created in [[1983]] called [[Wikipedia:American Flagg!|''American Flagg'']], a comic with more mature themes than most mainstream titles were depicting at the time.

He also drew the very first advance ''Star Wars'' poster.{{Mediacat|imagecat=Images by Howard Chaykin}}
